text: Literally, ‘the proud one’, but the word used in the Hebrew text is probably a proper name, Rahab. This is used as a title for Egypt, as in Ps. 88.11; and ‘the dragon’ is no doubt another symbol of the Egyptian power, as perhaps in Ps. 73.14.
From this verse it appears that the dialogue, verses 9-16, is either between the Lord and his prophet, or between the Lord and his Servant, cf. 49.2.
